remarks.name: Tigera Blog (Calico / AI Security)
remarks.role: AI agent governance, security, accountability frameworks, enterprise AI deployment policy.
remarks.text: Five Principles of an Accountable AI Agent Network: How to Evaluate Any Governance Platform
remarks.content: The first post in this series argued that AI agent governance hasn’t kept pace with deployment. The second laid out the five pillars of accountability, and what is required. The third walked through why network... The post Five Principles of an Accountable AI Agent Network: How to Evaluate Any
remarks.contentTranslation: 本系列的第一篇文章认为，AIAgent治理没有跟上部署的步伐。第二部分列出了问责制的五大支柱，以及需要做些什么。第三部分讲解了为什么要建立网络……《负责任的AIAgent网络的五项原则：如何评估
remarks.translation: 负责任的AIAgent网络的五大原则：如何评估任何治理平台
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Tigera Blog (Calico / AI Security)
remarks.role: AI agent governance, security, accountability frameworks, enterprise AI deployment policy.
remarks.text: A field guide to the agents in your cluster
remarks.content: You know every service in your cluster by name. You know which team owns each one, what it talks to, how it scales, where its logs go. The agents are a different story. That’s not... The post A field guide to the agents in your cluster appeared first on Tigera – Creator of Calico. ]]>
remarks.contentTranslation: 您可以通过名称了解集群中的每个服务。您知道哪个团队拥有每个团队，它谈论什么，它如何扩展，它的日志在哪里。经纪人是另一回事。这不是......帖子集群中特工的现场指南首先出现在Tigera – Calico的创建者。]] >
remarks.translation: 集群中客服代表的现场指南
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: Enterprise AI transformation: how leading organizations are redesigning operations around AI
remarks.content: Most enterprises have run AI pilots. But few have transformed their operations with AI at scale. What separates them is organizational design, not technology: disconnected people, disconnected systems, disconnected governance. According to "Global AI confessions report: data lead
remarks.contentTranslation: 大多数企业都运行AI试点。但很少有人用AI大规模地改变了他们的运营。将它们区分开来的是组织设计，而不是技术：断开的人员、断开的系统、断开的治理。根据《全球AI认罪报告：数据线索
remarks.translation: 企业AI转型：领先组织如何围绕AI重新设计运营
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: From partnership to pipeline: how Snowflake and Dataiku drove 200%+ YoY sourced opportunity growth
remarks.content: Partnerships are often judged too early, or on the wrong things. Visibility. Executive alignment. Event presence. A well-worded press release. None of that is the real test.
remarks.contentTranslation: 人们往往过早地判断伙伴关系，或者判断错误。可见性。高管一致性。事件存在。措辞良好的新闻稿。这些都不是真正的考验。
remarks.translation: 从合作伙伴关系到渠道： Snowflake和Dataiku如何推动200% +同比机会增长

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: Sovereignty isn't where your AI runs. It's whether it answers to you.
remarks.content: Last week, a government order took the world's best AI model offline in hours. No one deleted it and no server failed. A single directive put the most capable model on the market out of reach for everyone not a citizen of one country.
remarks.contentTranslation: 上周，一项政府命令在数小时内让世界上最好的AI模型下线。没有人将其删除，也没有服务器发生故障。单一指令使市场上最有能力的模型对每个人不是一个国家的公民来说都是遥不可及的。
remarks.translation: 主权并不是你的AI运行的地方。问题在于它是否对你有所回应。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: AI explainability in enterprise AI: methods, tools, and why it matters for trustworthy models and agents
remarks.content: AI models, generative AI (GenAI) applications, and agents are making decisions that affect credit scores, patient care, and hiring outcomes. Enterprises deploying these systems are accumulating regulatory and reputational risk proportional to the number of decisions that cannot be explai
remarks.contentTranslation: AI模型、生成式AI(GenAI)应用程序和Agent商正在做出影响信用评分、患者护理和招聘结果的决策。部署这些系统的企业正在积累与无法解释的决策数量成比例的监管和声誉风险
remarks.translation: 企业AI中的AI可解释性：方法、工具及其对可信模型和Agent的重要性
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: How enterprise analytics platforms are evolving: from dashboards to AI-driven decisions
remarks.content: The enterprise analytics platforms of 2023 were dashboard factories. The ones built for 2026 are decision engines where AI agents, machine learning (ML) models, and business rules operate alongside traditional reporting. But many enterprises still cannot route model outputs into decision
remarks.contentTranslation: 2023年的企业分析平台是仪表板工厂。为2026年构建的决策引擎是AIAgent、机器学习(ML)模型和业务规则与传统报告一起运行的决策引擎。但许多企业仍然无法将模型输出转化为决策
remarks.translation: 企业分析平台如何发展：从仪表板到AI驱动的决策
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Swyx
remarks.role: achieve ambition with intentionality, intensity, integrity & insanity.
remarks.text:
havent seen many people outside anthropic ultracode yet. this thing is scarily good at burning tokens but you need to set up your repo to parallelize properly to make use of the fanout that i think subagents are best at.

basically the idea is "subroutines but intelligent". when you undersatnd just how much knowledge work is just yakshaves after yakshaves that require some judgment and intelligence, you start to appreciate that dynamic workflows are not just for coding tasks...
remarks.translation:
在人类超代码之外还没有见过很多人。这个东西非常擅长燃烧令牌，但您需要设置存储库以正确并行化，以利用我认为子Agent最擅长的扇出。基本上，这个想法是“子程序但智能”。
当你低估了多少知识工作只是需要一些判断和智慧的yakshaves之后的yakshaves时，你开始意识到动态工作流不仅仅用于编码任务......
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.text: You make a skill by ending with one, not starting with one
remarks.translation: 您可以通过以一个结尾而不是以一个开头来创建技能
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Zara Zhang
remarks.role: Builder. Dangerously skips permissions. Harvard’17. GitHub: https://t.co/KCuEajezlL YouTube: https://t.co/8xzbGWtf6w
remarks.text:
You don't make a good skill by writing a skill. 

You make it by doing the thing, fixing it 20 times, then telling the AI to bottle up everything you just did.
remarks.translation: 你不能通过写技能来获得良好的技能。你可以通过做这件事，修复它20次，然后告诉AI把你刚刚做的所有事情都装进瓶子里。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
Great post. The companies that are able to get their unique IP, institutional knowledge, and data into a format and architecture that lets them capture all of the gains and progress in AI are going to be in the best position in the future.

“the real opportunity is not in picking the best model but instead in building a learning loop on top of models where human capital and token capital compound. You can offload a task, or even a job, but you can never offload your learning. The future of the firm is the ability to compound that learning across people and AI.

This requires a new architectural approach where every business is able to build agentic systems that improve over time, while still retaining control over their IP. A company should be able to switch out a “generalist” model without losing the “company veteran” expertise built into their learning system.”

We’re all collectively figuring out the right architecture for the future of AI. But it’s clear that so much of the power and value will accrue to wherever can best leverage any AI system against their information. This is also why the applied AI layer will also gain so much value over the coming years.
remarks.translation:
很棒的帖子。能够将其独特的知识产权、机构知识和数据转化为格式和架构，使他们能够捕捉AI领域的所有收益和进步的公司将在未来处于最佳位置。“真正的机会不是选择最好的模型，而是在人力资本和象征性资本混合的模型之上建立一个学习循环。
你可以卸载一项任务，甚至一项工作，但你永远不能卸载你的学习。公司的未来是能够将跨人和AI的学习结合起来。这需要一种新的架构方法，每个企业都能够构建随着时间的推移而改进的Agent系统，同时仍然保留对其IP的控制。
公司应该能够切换出“多面手”模式，而不会失去其学习系统中内置的“公司老兵”专业知识。“我们都在共同为AI的未来找出正确的架构。但很明显，无论在哪里，只要能最好地利用任何AI系统来处理他们的信息，就会积累如此多的权力和价值。这也是为什么应用的AI层在未来几年也将获得如此多的价值。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。

remarks.name: Swyx
remarks.role: achieve ambition with intentionality, intensity, integrity & insanity.
remarks.text:
Satya on loops as IP:
https://t.co/87HBsQptdQ
> This is the first time we can create a real cognitive loop between people and digital systems. That is a mind-bender, because it changes how we even conceptualize work inside an enterprise.

> This means the real opportunity is not in picking the best model but instead in building a learning loop on top of models where human capital and token capital compound. You can offload a task, or even a job, but you can never offload your learning

> In my view, our priority has to be building a frontier ecosystem, not just a frontier model, so value flows broadly across every company, every industry, and every country. One where every organization can own the learning loop that encodes its institutional knowledge, compounding its human and token capital.
remarks.translation:
Satya on loops as IP ： >这是我们第一次可以在人和数字系统之间创建一个真正的认知循环。这是一种思维颠覆，因为它甚至改变了我们对企业内部工作的概念化方式。>这意味着真正的机会不是选择最好的模型，而是在人力资本和象征性资本混合的模型之上建立一个学习循环。
你可以卸载一项任务，甚至一份工作，但你永远不能卸载你的学习>在我看来，我们的首要任务必须是建立一个前沿生态系统，而不仅仅是一个前沿模型，这样价值才能在每个公司、每个行业和每个国家广泛流动。每个组织都可以拥有编码其机构知识的学习循环，从而复合其人力和代币资本。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
The big winner in all of this is going to be open weights models. This is a huge win for the field, as a risk that was entirely theoretical and untested 2 days ago (that a model could be pulled back), now has a new precedent that’s been set.

The game theory the US should highly consider, and the risk with regulating AI at the model layer vs. applied layer, is that other countries now have even more incentive to develop sovereign AI.

If at any moment a model can be become unavailable to your country’s users or businesses, this poses very real risk on relying on technology from a particular country.

As a result, it forces major countries to charter their own path on AI development, which reduces America’s leadership role in this tech stack over time. The most likely solution that other countries will rely on is open weights models, which currently is generally not coming from the US.

America should be considering all of these downstream implications as it decides how and where in the stack to be regulating AI. At the same time, we should be doing a ton more OSS innovation.
remarks.translation:
所有这一切的最大赢家将是开放式权重模型。这对于该领域来说是一个巨大的胜利，因为2天前完全是理论和未经测试的风险（模型可以撤回） ，现在有了一个新的先例。美国应该高度考虑的博弈论，以及在模型层与应用层监管AI的风险，是其他国家现在更有动力开发主权AI。
如果您所在国家/地区的用户或企业可能随时无法使用某个模型，则依赖特定国家/地区的技术会带来非常现实的风险。因此，它迫使主要国家在AI发展方面开辟自己的道路，随着时间的推移，这削弱了美国在这一技术堆栈中的领导地位。其他国家最有可能依赖的解决方案是开放式权重模型，目前通常不是来自美国。
美国在决定如何以及在何处监管AI时，应该考虑所有这些下游影响。与此同时，我们应该做更多的OSS创新。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。
